title: Diagnose memory bugs with sanitizers and gdb/lldb
description: Rebuild with AddressSanitizer/UBSan, reproduce the crash, and use gdb/lldb to capture the faulting stack and the offending allocation.
body: Use when diagnosing a segfault, use-after-free, or undefined behavior. Actions: rebuild with -fsanitize=address,undefined -g -fno-omit-frame-pointer, rerun to get ASan's report (the access, the allocation site, and the free site), and if needed run under gdb/lldb to `bt` the faulting frame and inspect locals. Report the bug class, the file/line of the bad access, and the allocating/freeing stacks. Do not chase a release-build crash without a debug+sanitizer rebuild. This skill exposes capability only and must not attach to production processes without runtime permission. Validation signals include a reproducible ASan trace pinpointing the access and a clean rerun after the fix. Risk boundary: heap corruption can manifest far from its cause; if ASan and the crash site disagree, trust ASan's allocation trace and escalate rather than patching the symptom.
